assertion.title: Ternary mul3/muldiv/divmul operators reduce total nodes 263→252
assertion.statement: Adding ternary operator infrastructure with mul3(a,b,c)=a*b*c, muldiv(a,b,c)=a*b/c, and divmul(a,b,c)=a/(b*c) saves 11 nodes across 11 equations by compressing 3-variable arithmetic patterns, reducing total nodes from 263 to 252 while maintaining 50/50 exact matches.

details.hypothesis: Ternary operators for triple-product patterns (a*b*c, a*b/c, a/(b*c)) will reduce total node count by compressing the common 3-variable arithmetic patterns that appear across many physics equations.
changes: Added TERNARY_OPS dict with mul3, muldiv, divmul operators
Extended Node class with 'ternary' kind and __str__ support
Extended evaluate_tree to evaluate ternary nodes
Extended random_tree to occasionally generate ternary nodes (5% probability)
Extended point_mutate to swap between ternary operators
Added _make_ter helper for building ternary nodes
Added simplification rules: mul(X,mul(Y,Z))→mul3(X,Y,Z), div(mul(X,Y),Z)→muldiv(X,Y,Z), div(X,mul(Y,Z))→divmul(X,Y,Z) with commuted variants
details.key_insight: Three-variable products and quotients (a*b*c, a*b/c, a/(b*c)) are ubiquitous in physics: gravitational PE=mgh, work=Fdcosθ, ideal gas=nT/V, de Broglie=h/(mv). Binary trees require 5 nodes for 3-variable arithmetic (2 binary ops + 3 leaves). A single ternary operator reduces this to 4 nodes (1 ternary op + 3 leaves), saving 1 node per occurrence. The simplification rules automatically compress nested mul/div patterns, so existing templates benefit without modification.
